label: Structured outputs & typed action generation

node.label: Structured outputs & typed action generation
node.definition: Structured-output generation produces typed records, tool calls, plans, code, or commands that downstream software can parse and validate deterministically.
node.why_it_matters: It forms the contract boundary between probabilistic models and reliable software execution.
limitations: Valid structure can still contain incorrect, unsafe, unauthorized, or semantically inconsistent values.

dependencies.rationale: Structured outputs & typed action generation uses Grammar- and schema-constrained decoding as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Structured outputs & typed action generation uses Structured-output parsing, validation & repair as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Structured outputs & typed action generation uses Tool use & function calling as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Structured outputs & typed action generation uses Schemas, grammars & formal output languages as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
